macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Stock Futures Surge on Trump Iran Suspension; Oil Prices Fall
items.summary: U.S. stock futures rose sharply after President Trump announced a two-week suspension of Iran attacks, contingent on agreements, while oil prices declined amid reduced geopolitical tensions. Markets showed resilience in tech and utilities despite uncertainties.

items.title: Markets Brace for Trump Iran Deadline; Dow Drops 250 Points
items.summary: Global markets face uncertainty ahead of President Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with the Dow down 250 points and oil risks looming. Investors seek safety amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

items.title: New York Fed Lowers Economic Growth Forecast Due to Middle East Conflict
items.summary: New York Fed President John Williams reduced his economic growth forecast for the year, citing ongoing Middle East conflicts. This reflects broader concerns over regional instability impacting U.S. outlook.

items.title: Fed's Jefferson: Economy Growing at Potential, Inflation at 2.8% PCE
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Jefferson stated the U.S. economy is growing in line with potential, led by consumer spending and business investment, but inflation remains above 2% target at 2.8% PCE and core at 3.0%. Progress stalled due to tariffs and elevated energy prices.

items.title: ECB Policymaker Warns of Rising Inflation Expectations
items.summary: Euro zone inflation expectations risk rising quickly, prompting calls for the ECB to be ready to raise rates swiftly if persistent price pressures emerge. This highlights vigilance against inflation scars.

items.title: U.S. Dollar Index Rises on Energy Prices and Safe-Haven Demand
items.summary: The U.S. Dollar Index gained in early trading, supported by stabilizing energy prices, U.S. labor markets, and safe-haven flows amid global uncertainties.

items.title: Texas Stock Exchange Set for July 2026 Launch
items.summary: Texas Governor Greg Abbott discussed the upcoming July 2026 opening of the Texas Stock Exchange, positioning Texas as a new financial power center challenging Wall Street.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $72,000 on US-Iran Ceasefire Announcement
items.summary: Bitcoin jumped up to 4.43% to $71,709-$72,000, Ethereum rose 6.54% to $2,243, XRP gained 5.38% to $1.38, and Solana climbed 1.8% to $84.51 following President Trump's two-week suspension of attacks on Iran and ceasefire agreement.[1][3][4][6]

items.title: Top Crypto Gainers: ORDER (ORDI) Leads with 9.48% Surge
items.summary: ORDER (ORDI) topped 24-hour gainers at 9.48% to $0.0587 with $16.76M volume, followed by MOG (+3.57%), TREE (+3.03%), 0G, and CPOOL amid broader market recovery.[2]

items.title: Bitcoin-Dominated Crypto Futures Liquidations Hit $245.7 Million
items.summary: Crypto perpetual futures saw $245.70 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, led by Bitcoin, amid volatile price swings from the US-Iran ceasefire news.[5]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Hits $30 Billion Run Rate as Enterprise Demand Accelerates
items.summary: Anthropic's valuation has reached a $30 billion run rate, driven by increasing enterprise adoption of its AI services and models.

items.title: Anthropic Unleashes Mythos AI Model in Project Glasswing Security Initiative
items.summary: Anthropic has deployed its powerful Mythos AI model through Project Glasswing to identify and reinforce software security vulnerabilities in a high-stakes bug hunt.

items.title: Intel Joins Elon Musk's $25 Billion Terafab AI Chip Project
items.summary: Intel has partnered with Elon Musk's Terafab initiative to develop AI chips for powering humanoid robots and data center applications.

items.title: Google Updates Gemini with Enhanced Mental Health and Self-Harm Safeguards
items.summary: Google has rolled out new safety measures in its Gemini AI model to better address suicide and self-harm concerns as AI-related lawsuits mount.

items.title: FBI Reports $893 Million in AI-Driven Scams
items.summary: Federal authorities have flagged nearly $900 million in fraudulent activities powered by artificial intelligence technology.

items.title: IBM Expands FedRAMP Authorization for 11 AI and Automation Solutions
items.summary: IBM announced that 11 of its AI software solutions, including watsonx products, have received Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program authorization for government use.

items.title: ElevenLabs and IBM Collaborate to Enhance Agentic AI with Premium Voice Capabilities
items.summary: ElevenLabs and IBM have partnered to integrate advanced text-to-speech and speech-to-text capabilities into IBM's watsonx Orchestrate platform for enterprise AI applications.

items.title: Modus Secures $85 Million to Expand AI-Powered Audit and Accounting Services
items.summary: Modus has raised $85 million in funding to scale its AI-driven audit and accounting partnership offerings.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie's Marathon Flops with Only 1.2 Million Copies Sold
items.summary: Marathon has reportedly sold just 1.2 million copies, far below expectations of 7 million, leading to player count drops below decade-old games. Despite the failure, developers promise years of content and no immediate shutdown like Concord.

items.title: Surprise Call of Duty Update Adds New Weapon and Modern Warfare News
items.summary: A new surprise update for Black Ops 7 and Warzone introduces a new weapon, fixes, and additional Modern Warfare developments ahead of MW4's release later this year.

items.title: Wardogs Announces Early Access Plans with 350,000 Wishlisters
items.summary: Indie game Wardogs, described as Arma 3's King of the Hill mode expanded, reveals devlog on economy, skills, wipes, and gameplay, preparing for a strong early access launch later this year despite expected player drop-off.

items.title: Clash of Clans Launches Sound of Clash Event with New Epic Equipment
items.summary: The Sound of Clash medal event for Town Hall 6+ players runs from April 8 to 29, introducing the Rocket Backpack epic equipment for Dragon Duke and Spiky Bracelets rewards.

items.title: Warzone Pro Scrims Feature Gen.G and New Teammate
items.summary: Live Warzone professional scrims ongoing with team Gen.G and a new teammate, highlighting competitive esports activity.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Discontinuing Its Texting App, Directing Users to Google Messages
items.summary: Samsung is ending support for its native texting app and instructing affected users to switch to Google Messages. This move aligns with broader industry shifts toward unified messaging solutions.

items.title: Cloudflare Accelerates Quantum-Resistant Encryption Deadline to 2029
items.summary: Following new research from Google and Oratomic on faster quantum computing advances, Cloudflare announced it is speeding up preparations for quantum-safe encryption to 2029, ahead of NIST's 2035 target.

items.title: New Software Nearly Doubles Pooled SSD Performance in Data Centers
items.summary: Researchers have developed software that can almost double the performance of pooled SSDs in data center environments, enhancing storage efficiency.

items.title: NASA and JAXA Test X-59 Supersonic Model in Mach 1.4 Wind Tunnel
items.summary: NASA and JAXA conducted wind tunnel tests on a hand-sized model of the X-59 quiet supersonic aircraft at Mach 1.4, advancing development for low-boom commercial flight.
